# Seed Contract

**Format name:** Seed file (`docs/seeds/<filename>`)
**Filename:** generated by `scripts/doc-filename.sh <slug> json` → `YYYYMMDD-HHMM-{slug}.json`
**Schema:** `contracts/seed-schema.json` (schema_version: `"4"`)

## Producers

- `skills/to-seed/` — distills a conversation or grill session into a seed file
- `skills/prototype/` — conditional producer; writes a seed file when user chooses option 1 in the end-of-run save prompt

## Consumers

- `skills/to-tasks/` — reads seed file (or HTML PRD) to generate a task file
- `skills/to-prd-html/` — reads seed file and renders it into an HTML PRD (`docs/prd/YYYYMMDD-HHMM-{slug}.html`, filename via `scripts/doc-filename.sh <slug> html`)

## HTML PRD: rendered seed format

`to-prd-html` produces HTML PRDs that embed the seed JSON. `to-tasks` can consume them as an alternate input via `scripts/extract-prd-json.sh <path>`, which extracts the embedded seed JSON from the HTML. This makes the chain: seed → HTML PRD → tasks equally valid alongside seed → tasks directly.

HTML PRDs are not a separately versioned schema. They carry the seed JSON verbatim inside the HTML, so the seed-schema.json constraint applies transitively.

## Schema file

[seed-schema.json](seed-schema.json)

## v4 field additions

**`iteration`** (integer, optional): Number of grill/verification cycles this seed has gone through. Telemetry only.

**`open_threads`** (object array, was `string[]`): Each entry is `{id, text, first_seen_iteration}`. The `id` is a stable slug (e.g. `ot-001`) that survives disposal and is referenced by `disposed_threads`.

**`disposed_threads`** (object array, optional): Lock list of threads that were resolved, deferred, or rejected during grilling. Each entry is `{id, text, disposition, iteration}`. Grill resume mode reads this list and blocks any thread with a matching id from being re-raised.

**`verification`** (object, optional): Stamp written by `to-seed` after the adversary panel run. Shape: `{iteration, personas[], refutations_upheld, clean, status}`. `status` is `"verified"` on a clean or corrected pass and `"degraded"` when the panel did not fully complete. `to-tasks` hard-refuses seeds with `verification.status === "degraded"` when `schema_version` is `"4"`. Seeds without a `verification` field (v3 and earlier) are accepted without a check.
